ASSERTION GAP: Current assertions miss the most critical difference. With_skill always produces skeleton (pass bodies); baseline always generates full implementation. Both score identically (0.841) because no assertion checks method body content. Add 'methods-are-skeleton' assertion in iteration 2.

main-py-is-thin fails for BOTH configurations every time — it is non-discriminating on pass/fail (always fails) but does capture magnitude: with_skill 17-20 lines vs baseline 24-41 lines. Split into two: 'no-logic-in-main' (discriminating) and 'line-count-reasonable' (threshold should be ~20, not 15).
no-optional-syntax passes for both in all evals — non-discriminating, can be removed or hardened.
With_skill uses ~22% more tokens (18583 vs 15222 mean) — cost of reading skill instructions.
With_skill README quality is lower than baseline (40 vs 78 lines avg). Skill should set a minimum README length expectation.
